sentence: Best in Class organizations succeed at outsourcing IT functions in two ways: by devoting as much management time and effort to their relationships with third-party providers and by not being timid in going to the providers when service levels fall short of expectations.

One startling statistic that came out of Aberdeen’s recent √ Get the RFP right. survey for _The Business Value of IT_ √ Don’t be afraid to look outside for help. _Outsourcing Benchmark Report_ : Fully √ Improve relationship management. 25% of mid-market respondents admitted their companies have not been successful
None of the Best in Class cited a lack of success.

Figure 2 highlights the differences in how mid-size and Best in Class companies overcome outsourcing challenges.
**Figure 2: How Companies Overcome Challenges to IT Outsourcing**
**Don’t be afraid to look outside for help.** Most Best in Class companies rely on independent consulting firms.
Many of the Best in Class, more so than mid-size enterprises represented in our survey, bank on specialized outside help, such as legal assistance, before executing a contract.
**Improve relationship management.** Most of the Best in Class devoted more management time and effort to nurturing and improving relationships with outsourcers.
